title: Today's Markets Brief: SpaceX Sets $135 IPO Price
summary: SpaceX's blockbuster IPO, after-hours stock movers, and market trends.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your markets brief. I've got my eyes on some big news today. SpaceX, the Elon Musk-led space exploration company, has set its IPO price at $135 per share. That's a significant number, especially when you consider the company's valuation. This move is upending Wall Street convention, and I think it's worth exploring why. To me, it seems like a bold move, and I'm curious to see how it will play out.

According to Investing.com, SpaceX's $135 IPO price is making waves. The company's valuation is now being closely watched, and investors are eager to see how this will impact the market. I am looking at the numbers, and it's clear that SpaceX is confident in its growth prospects. The question is, what does this mean for the rest of the market? It's also worth noting that this high-profile IPO could set the tone for other companies looking to go public.

In after-hours trading, several stocks are making moves. AVGO, CRWD, FIVE, AI, TLYS, and NTSK are all seeing significant action. It's always interesting to see which companies are getting attention outside of regular trading hours. My read is that investors are looking for opportunities, and these stocks are catching their eye. Some of these companies might be experiencing volatility, while others might be riding a wave of positive news.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.

sources.title: SpaceX sets $135 price for blockbuster IPO, upending Wall Street convention
